content: Hi, guys. I currently work on write tty0tty module in rust, but there is no tty support on R4L, it seems i need to realize it by myself. I found a tutorial (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3VU0hfsbHdc) but it is out of date and it work on the old rust branch. Is there any new tutorial for beginer?
is anyone has any idea about it?
Are you just looking for how to get started building a driver? If so, a good place is to start with the out of tree module https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/rust-out-of-tree-module and go from there
There may not be enough API merged for tty, depending on what you want to do, but when you find things that don't work we can point you in the correct direction
i know there is not enough API merged for tty, i want to implement it by myself, how can i do that?
i know i need use bindgen, and add safe abstract in the rust/kernel. However, it is quite difficult for a beginner, is there any example(better tutorial) on how to add rust support for any driver?

@wenzhao: Rust support in the kernel is still very new, so many things are missing, including step by step guides. Most people here are getting started by reading the code from other people from the Mailing List or from their development branches.
Since most of the things for driver development are not upstream, Rust APIs can change quickly and it would not be useful to have a step by step guide for getting started with driver development. Though as the Device Driver core abstraction is getting merged, there is hope that more things goes in soon and things stabilize.

If you need some inspiration about some "simple" device drivers with the subsystem abstractions needed, I have a couple of branches that you can look into that might help you get started:

  • https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commits/fparent/rust-ncv6336/
  • https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commits/fparent/rust-truly-r63350

For bindgen in particular, you can look at this I2C commit in the first branch I shared: https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commit/8bf3e991e9f15a85560f001f56ef25ea814c0fc6
thanks for your help, that's very helpful.
wenzhao has marked this topic as resolved.
